content.question: The actual magnitude of net positive equity gain in Hungary from delayed selection (holding school-choice and catchment rules constant) remains unknown; Polish gains may not generalize given Hungary's significantly higher baseline segregation and denser network of small rural schools.
content.reduction_path: A quasi-experimental pilot in one or more Hungarian regions (comparing delayed-selection cohorts to propensity-matched controls on standardized achievement measures, teacher supply, and between/within-school segregation) over 5–7 years.
